variables.$fxv#0:
<!--
    Service-level policy applied to every API on this APIM instance.
    Adds CORS so browser-based demos / web SPAs can call the gateway.
    Tightening for production: scope `allowed-origins` to your real domains.
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <!-- DEMO-ONLY CORS: allow-origin="*" lets any web page call the
             gateway. Tighten the <allowed-origins> list to your real domains
             before promoting this configuration past a sandbox. -->
        <cors allow-credentials="false">
            <allowed-origins>
                <origin>*</origin>
            </allowed-origins>
            <allowed-methods preflight-result-max-age="3600">
                <method>GET</method>
                <method>POST</method>
                <method>OPTIONS</method>
            </allowed-methods>
            <allowed-headers>
                <header>*</header>
            </allowed-headers>
            <expose-headers>
                <header>x-ai-gateway-cache</header>
                <header>x-ai-gateway-fallback</header>
                <header>x-ai-gateway-route</header>
                <header>x-request-id</header>
            </expose-headers>
        </cors>
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <forward-request />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
    </on-error>
</policies>

metadata.description: Existing APIM service name.

variables.$fxv#0:
<!--
    Fragment: openai-usage
    Purpose:  Capture token usage for non-streaming Azure OpenAI responses and
              emit it as a structured trace into Application Insights so it can
              be queried with KQL for per-app / per-route / per-region
              charge-back.

    Pre-conditions (set by the API-level policy):
        - `isStream` (true|false)
        - `apiRoute` (e.g. "global" | "aue")
        - `apiRegion` (e.g. "australiaeast" | "global")
        - `appId`, `useCase`, `backendId`, `deployment-id`, `routeLocation`
-->
<fragment>
    <choose>
        <when condition="@(context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("isStream","false").Equals("false", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))">
            <set-variable name="responseBody" value="@(context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true))" />

            <choose>
                <when condition="@(context.Response.StatusCode == 200)">
                    <trace source="ai-gateway" severity="information">
                        <message>@("ai-gateway-token-usage")</message>
                        <metadata name="appId" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("appId", "unknown"))" />
                        <metadata name="useCase" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("useCase", "unknown"))" />
                        <metadata name="apiRoute" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute", "unknown"))" />
                        <metadata name="apiRegion" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRegion", "unknown"))" />
                        <metadata name="subscriptionId" value="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")" />
                        <metadata name="subscriptionName" value="@(context.Subscription?.Name ?? "anonymous")" />
                        <metadata name="productId" value="@(context.Product?.Id ?? "none")" />
                        <metadata name="productName" value="@(context.Product?.Name ?? "none")" />
                        <metadata name="apiId" value="@(context.Api?.Id ?? "none")" />
                        <metadata name="operationName" value="@(context.Operation?.Name ?? "none")" />
                        <metadata name="deploymentName" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("deployment-id", "none"))" />
                        <metadata name="backendId" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("backendId", "default"))" />
                        <metadata name="routeLocation" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("routeLocation", "none"))" />
                        <metadata name="model" value="@(((JObject)context.Variables["responseBody"])["model"]?.ToString() ?? "unknown")" />
                        <metadata name="promptTokens" value="@(((JObject)context.Variables["responseBody"])?["usage"]?["prompt_tokens"]?.Value<int>().ToString() ?? "0")" />
                        <metadata name="completionTokens" value="@(((JObject)context.Variables["responseBody"])?["usage"]?["completion_tokens"]?.Value<int>().ToString() ?? "0")" />
                        <metadata name="totalTokens" value="@(((JObject)context.Variables["responseBody"])?["usage"]?["total_tokens"]?.Value<int>().ToString() ?? "0")" />
                        <metadata name="clientIp" value="@(context.Request.IpAddress ?? "0.0.0.0")" />
                        <metadata name="sessionId" value="@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-session-id", "none"))" />
                        <metadata name="endUserId" value="@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-user-id", "none"))" />
                        <metadata name="gatewayRegion" value="@(context.Deployment?.Region ?? "none")" />
                    </trace>
                </when>
            </choose>
        </when>
    </choose>
</fragment>
variables.$fxv#1:
<!--
    Fragment: throttling-events
    Purpose:  When the upstream Azure OpenAI service returns 429 (rate limit)
              the on-error block calls this fragment to write a structured trace
              into Application Insights so an alert / KQL query can spot
              service degradation per app / product / deployment.
-->
<fragment>
    <choose>
        <when condition="@(context.Response != null && context.Response.StatusCode == 429)">
            <trace source="ai-gateway" severity="error">
                <message>@("ai-gateway-throttling-event")</message>
                <metadata name="appId" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("appId", "unknown"))" />
                <metadata name="useCase" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("useCase", "unknown"))" />
                <metadata name="subscriptionName" value="@(context.Subscription?.Name ?? "anonymous")" />
                <metadata name="productName" value="@(context.Product?.Name ?? "none")" />
                <metadata name="deploymentName" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("deployment-id", "none"))" />
                <metadata name="backendId" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("backendId", "default"))" />
                <metadata name="retryAfter" value="@(context.Response.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("Retry-After", "n/a"))" />
                <metadata name="statusCode" value="@(context.Response.StatusCode.ToString())" />
                <metadata name="reason" value="@(context.Response.StatusReason ?? "throttled")" />
            </trace>
        </when>
    </choose>
</fragment>
variables.$fxv#2:
<!--
    Fragment: cache-lookup
    Purpose:  Lightweight response cache for non-streaming chat completions.
              Computes a SHA-256 over (deployment-id + product + body) so identical
              requests from the same product return immediately without hitting
              the model. Demonstrates APIM's built-in cache without external
              infra (no Redis required for the Developer SKU internal cache).

    NOTE: This is a body-hash cache, not a semantic cache. Semantic cache needs
          `azure-openai-semantic-cache-lookup` + Redis Enterprise + an embeddings
          deployment; out of scope for this single-Foundry demo.
-->
<fragment>
    <choose>
        <when condition="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("isStream","false") == "false")">
            <set-variable name="cacheKey" value="@{
                var bodyText = context.Request.Body.As<string>(preserveContent: true);
                // Cache key isolation: include the calling APIM subscription
                // id so two different subscriptions never see each other's
                // cached responses, even within the same product.
                var raw = (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("deployment-id","unknown")
                        + "|" + (context.Product?.Id ?? "none")
                        + "|" + (context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")
                        + "|" + bodyText;
                using (var sha = System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256.Create())
                {
                    var bytes = sha.ComputeHash(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(raw));
                    return "ai-gw:" + Convert.ToBase64String(bytes);
                }
            }" />
            <cache-lookup-value key="@((string)context.Variables["cacheKey"])" variable-name="cachedBody" />
            <choose>
                <when condition="@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("cachedBody"))">
                    <return-response>
                        <set-status code="200" reason="OK (cached)" />
                        <set-header name="content-type" exists-action="override">
                            <value>application/json</value>
                        </set-header>
                        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-cache" exists-action="override">
                            <value>HIT</value>
                        </set-header>
                        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-route" exists-action="override">
                            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute","unknown"))</value>
                        </set-header>
                        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-region" exists-action="override">
                            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRegion","unknown"))</value>
                        </set-header>
                        <set-body>@((string)context.Variables["cachedBody"])</set-body>
                    </return-response>
                </when>
            </choose>
        </when>
    </choose>
</fragment>
variables.$fxv#3:
<!--
    Fragment: cache-store
    Purpose:  Persist successful non-streaming chat-completion responses into
              the APIM internal cache for 5 minutes, keyed by the same SHA-256
              hash computed in `frag-cache-lookup`.
-->
<fragment>
    <choose>
        <when condition="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("isStream","false") == "false"
                            && context.Response.StatusCode == 200
                            && context.Variables.ContainsKey("cacheKey")
                            && context.Variables.ContainsKey("responseBody"))">
            <cache-store-value
                key="@((string)context.Variables["cacheKey"])"
                value="@(((Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["responseBody"]).ToString())"
                duration="300" />
            <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-cache" exists-action="override">
                <value>MISS</value>
            </set-header>
        </when>
    </choose>
</fragment>
variables.$fxv#4:
<!--
    Fragment: mock-fallback
    Purpose:  When the upstream model backend fails (5xx or circuit breaker
              open), instead of bubbling the raw error up to the caller we
              return a structured, branded fallback response so client apps
              degrade gracefully and customers see APIM's resilience.

              Triggers only on 5xx (not 429 - those are real throttling and
              the caller should retry; we still log them via frag-throttling
              -events).
-->
<fragment>
    <choose>
        <when condition="@(context.Response != null && context.Response.StatusCode >= 500)">
            <return-response>
                <set-status code="503" reason="Service Unavailable" />
                <set-header name="content-type" exists-action="override">
                    <value>application/json</value>
                </set-header>
                <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-fallback" exists-action="override">
                    <value>mock</value>
                </set-header>
                <set-body>@{
                    var payload = new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject(
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("error", new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject(
                            new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("code", "ai_gateway_backend_unavailable"),
                            new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("message", "The AI Gateway could not reach a healthy model backend. Please retry shortly."),
                            new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("appId", (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("appId","unknown")),
                            new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("useCase", (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("useCase","unknown")),
                            new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("route", (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute","unknown")),
                            new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("requestId", context.RequestId)
                        ))
                    );
                    return payload.ToString();
                }</set-body>
            </return-response>
        </when>
    </choose>
</fragment>

properties.description: Captures OpenAI token usage as a structured trace.
Captures 429 throttling events as warning traces.
Body-hash response cache lookup; short-circuits on HIT.
Persists successful non-streaming responses for 5 minutes.
Returns a graceful 503 mock response when the backend pool fails.

variables.apiPolicyTemplate:
<!--
    API-level policy for both "Azure OpenAI - Australia East" and
    "Azure OpenAI - Global" APIs. The same policy is applied to both APIs;
    the per-API differences (route name, region label) come from variables
    substituted by Bicep before the policy is uploaded.

    Demonstrated capabilities:
      1. Subscription-key auth at the gateway (`api-key` header).
      2. JSON request validation - chat completions must contain a
         non-empty `messages` array.
      3. Body-hash response cache (frag-cache-lookup) -> short-circuit.
      4. Streaming detection.
      5. Drop the caller key, use APIM MI to auth to Foundry.
      6. Per-subscription TPM ceiling via `azure-openai-token-limit`.
      7. Native token metric emission with `azure-openai-emit-token-metric`
         (App ID / Use Case / Product / Deployment / Route / Region).
      8. Backend POOL routing (priority + circuit breaker, set in Bicep)
         with automatic failover.
      9. Streaming-aware response forwarding.
     10. Outbound: structured trace per call (frag-openai-usage).
     11. Outbound: cache-store for repeat-request acceleration.
     12. on-error: graceful mock fallback for 5xx, throttling-event trace
         for 429.
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />

        <!-- ============================================================
             Per-API metadata. The Bicep substitutes the values below per
             API instance via `replace()` before pushing the policy.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="apiRoute" value="__API_ROUTE__" />
        <set-variable name="apiRegion" value="__API_REGION__" />
        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-route" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["apiRoute"])</value>
        </set-header>

        <!-- ============================================================
             1. Capture deployment id from URL template.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="deployment-id" value="@(context.Request.MatchedParameters.GetValueOrDefault("deployment-id", "unknown"))" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             1a. Reject query-string subscription keys. APIM's default API
                 config still honours `?subscription-key=...` even when we
                 leave it out of `subscriptionKeyParameterNames` in Bicep.
                 Query-string keys are easier to leak via referrers / logs,
                 so we enforce header-only at the policy layer.
             ============================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(!string.IsNullOrEmpty(context.Request.Url.Query.GetValueOrDefault("subscription-key","")))">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="400" reason="Bad Request" />
                    <set-header name="content-type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>{"error":{"code":"ai_gateway_query_key_disabled","message":"Subscription keys must be passed in the 'api-key' header. Query-string keys are disabled to avoid leaking credentials via referrers and access logs."}}</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- ============================================================
             2. Lightweight request validation - reject malformed chat
                completion requests at the gateway so we don't waste
                backend capacity / TPM budget.
             ============================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@{
                try {
                    var body = context.Request.Body?.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                    if (body == null) { return true; }
                    var messages = body["messages"] as JArray;
                    return messages == null || messages.Count == 0;
                } catch { return true; }
            }">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="400" reason="Bad Request" />
                    <set-header name="content-type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>{"error":{"code":"ai_gateway_invalid_request","message":"Request body must include a non-empty 'messages' array."}}</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- ============================================================
             3. Detect streaming early - cache + buffering decisions
                depend on it.
             ============================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@{
                try {
                    var body = context.Request.Body?.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                    return body != null
                        && body["stream"] != null
                        && body["stream"].Type != JTokenType.Null
                        && body["stream"].Value<bool>();
                } catch { return false; }
            }">
                <set-variable name="isStream" value="true" />
            </when>
            <otherwise>
                <set-variable name="isStream" value="false" />
            </otherwise>
        </choose>

        <!-- ============================================================
             4. Response cache lookup (non-streaming only). On HIT the
                fragment short-circuits with a `return-response`.
             ============================================================ -->
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-cache-lookup" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             5. Strip the caller's gateway keys so they never reach Foundry.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-header name="api-key" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key" exists-action="delete" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             6. Authenticate to Azure OpenAI / Foundry using APIM's MI.
             ============================================================ -->
        <authentication-managed-identity
            resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com"
            output-token-variable-name="msi-access-token"
            ignore-error="false" />
        <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="override">
            <value>@("Bearer " + (string)context.Variables["msi-access-token"])</value>
        </set-header>

        <!-- ============================================================
             7. Per-subscription TPM safety net (the per-product policy
                layers a tighter limit on top of this).
             ============================================================ -->
        <azure-openai-token-limit
            counter-key="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")"
            tokens-per-minute="50000"
            estimate-prompt-tokens="false"
            tokens-consumed-variable-name="consumedTokens"
            remaining-tokens-variable-name="remainingTokens" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             8. Native Application Insights token metrics with rich
                charge-back dimensions (incl. Route + Region).
             ============================================================ -->
        <azure-openai-emit-token-metric namespace="ai-gateway">
            <dimension name="Subscription ID" value="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")" />
            <dimension name="Subscription Name" value="@(context.Subscription?.Name ?? "anonymous")" />
            <dimension name="Product Name" value="@(context.Product?.Name ?? "none")" />
            <dimension name="App ID" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("appId", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Use Case" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("useCase", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Deployment" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("deployment-id", "none"))" />
            <dimension name="API ID" value="@(context.Api?.Id ?? "none")" />
            <dimension name="Route" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Region Label" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRegion", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Client IP" value="@(context.Request.IpAddress ?? "0.0.0.0")" />
        </azure-openai-emit-token-metric>

        <!-- ============================================================
             9. Route to the backend POOL. The pool (created in Bicep) has
                two members with different priorities + circuit breakers,
                so APIM transparently fails over when a member is unhealthy.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-backend-service backend-id="openai-backend-pool" />
        <set-variable name="backendId" value="openai-backend-pool" />
        <set-variable name="routeLocation" value="@(context.Deployment?.Region ?? "none")" />
    </inbound>

    <backend>
        <choose>
            <when condition="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("isStream", "false") == "true")">
                <forward-request buffer-response="false" />
            </when>
            <otherwise>
                <forward-request buffer-response="true" />
            </otherwise>
        </choose>
    </backend>

    <outbound>
        <base />
        <!-- Add route + region headers so SDK callers see APIM provenance -->
        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-route" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute","unknown"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-region" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRegion","unknown"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <!-- Per-request usage trace + cache-store for non-streaming responses -->
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-openai-usage" />
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-cache-store" />
    </outbound>

    <on-error>
        <base />
        <!-- Capture 429s as warnings before optionally short-circuiting on 5xx -->
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-throttling-events" />
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-mock-fallback" />
    </on-error>
</policies>

apiDescription.value: Azure OpenAI route intended for non-residency workloads. Routes via the AI Gateway pool with global priority.

<!--
    API-level policy for both "Azure OpenAI - Australia East" and
    "Azure OpenAI - Global" APIs. The same policy is applied to both APIs;
    the per-API differences (route name, region label) come from variables
    substituted by Bicep before the policy is uploaded.

    Demonstrated capabilities:
      1. Subscription-key auth at the gateway (`api-key` header).
      2. JSON request validation - chat completions must contain a
         non-empty `messages` array.
      3. Body-hash response cache (frag-cache-lookup) -> short-circuit.
      4. Streaming detection.
      5. Drop the caller key, use APIM MI to auth to Foundry.
      6. Per-subscription TPM ceiling via `azure-openai-token-limit`.
      7. Native token metric emission with `azure-openai-emit-token-metric`
         (App ID / Use Case / Product / Deployment / Route / Region).
      8. Backend POOL routing (priority + circuit breaker, set in Bicep)
         with automatic failover.
      9. Streaming-aware response forwarding.
     10. Outbound: structured trace per call (frag-openai-usage).
     11. Outbound: cache-store for repeat-request acceleration.
     12. on-error: graceful mock fallback for 5xx, throttling-event trace
         for 429.
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />

        <!-- ============================================================
             Per-API metadata. The Bicep substitutes the values below per
             API instance via `replace()` before pushing the policy.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="apiRoute" value="__API_ROUTE__" />
        <set-variable name="apiRegion" value="__API_REGION__" />
        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-route" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["apiRoute"])</value>
        </set-header>

        <!-- ============================================================
             1. Capture deployment id from URL template.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="deployment-id" value="@(context.Request.MatchedParameters.GetValueOrDefault("deployment-id", "unknown"))" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             1a. Reject query-string subscription keys. APIM's default API
                 config still honours `?subscription-key=...` even when we
                 leave it out of `subscriptionKeyParameterNames` in Bicep.
                 Query-string keys are easier to leak via referrers / logs,
                 so we enforce header-only at the policy layer.
             ============================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(!string.IsNullOrEmpty(context.Request.Url.Query.GetValueOrDefault("subscription-key","")))">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="400" reason="Bad Request" />
                    <set-header name="content-type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>{"error":{"code":"ai_gateway_query_key_disabled","message":"Subscription keys must be passed in the 'api-key' header. Query-string keys are disabled to avoid leaking credentials via referrers and access logs."}}</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- ============================================================
             2. Lightweight request validation - reject malformed chat
                completion requests at the gateway so we don't waste
                backend capacity / TPM budget.
             ============================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@{
                try {
                    var body = context.Request.Body?.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                    if (body == null) { return true; }
                    var messages = body["messages"] as JArray;
                    return messages == null || messages.Count == 0;
                } catch { return true; }
            }">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="400" reason="Bad Request" />
                    <set-header name="content-type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>{"error":{"code":"ai_gateway_invalid_request","message":"Request body must include a non-empty 'messages' array."}}</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- ============================================================
             3. Detect streaming early - cache + buffering decisions
                depend on it.
             ============================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@{
                try {
                    var body = context.Request.Body?.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                    return body != null
                        && body["stream"] != null
                        && body["stream"].Type != JTokenType.Null
                        && body["stream"].Value<bool>();
                } catch { return false; }
            }">
                <set-variable name="isStream" value="true" />
            </when>
            <otherwise>
                <set-variable name="isStream" value="false" />
            </otherwise>
        </choose>

        <!-- ============================================================
             4. Response cache lookup (non-streaming only). On HIT the
                fragment short-circuits with a `return-response`.
             ============================================================ -->
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-cache-lookup" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             5. Strip the caller's gateway keys so they never reach Foundry.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-header name="api-key" exists-action="delete" />
        <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key" exists-action="delete" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             6. Authenticate to Azure OpenAI / Foundry using APIM's MI.
             ============================================================ -->
        <authentication-managed-identity
            resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com"
            output-token-variable-name="msi-access-token"
            ignore-error="false" />
        <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="override">
            <value>@("Bearer " + (string)context.Variables["msi-access-token"])</value>
        </set-header>

        <!-- ============================================================
             7. Per-subscription TPM safety net (the per-product policy
                layers a tighter limit on top of this).
             ============================================================ -->
        <azure-openai-token-limit
            counter-key="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")"
            tokens-per-minute="50000"
            estimate-prompt-tokens="false"
            tokens-consumed-variable-name="consumedTokens"
            remaining-tokens-variable-name="remainingTokens" />

        <!-- ============================================================
             8. Native Application Insights token metrics with rich
                charge-back dimensions (incl. Route + Region).
             ============================================================ -->
        <azure-openai-emit-token-metric namespace="ai-gateway">
            <dimension name="Subscription ID" value="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")" />
            <dimension name="Subscription Name" value="@(context.Subscription?.Name ?? "anonymous")" />
            <dimension name="Product Name" value="@(context.Product?.Name ?? "none")" />
            <dimension name="App ID" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("appId", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Use Case" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("useCase", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Deployment" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("deployment-id", "none"))" />
            <dimension name="API ID" value="@(context.Api?.Id ?? "none")" />
            <dimension name="Route" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Region Label" value="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRegion", "unknown"))" />
            <dimension name="Client IP" value="@(context.Request.IpAddress ?? "0.0.0.0")" />
        </azure-openai-emit-token-metric>

        <!-- ============================================================
             9. Route to the backend POOL. The pool (created in Bicep) has
                two members with different priorities + circuit breakers,
                so APIM transparently fails over when a member is unhealthy.
             ============================================================ -->
        <set-backend-service backend-id="openai-backend-pool" />
        <set-variable name="backendId" value="openai-backend-pool" />
        <set-variable name="routeLocation" value="@(context.Deployment?.Region ?? "none")" />
    </inbound>

    <backend>
        <choose>
            <when condition="@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("isStream", "false") == "true")">
                <forward-request buffer-response="false" />
            </when>
            <otherwise>
                <forward-request buffer-response="true" />
            </otherwise>
        </choose>
    </backend>

    <outbound>
        <base />
        <!-- Add route + region headers so SDK callers see APIM provenance -->
        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-route" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRoute","unknown"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-ai-gateway-region" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<string>("apiRegion","unknown"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <!-- Per-request usage trace + cache-store for non-streaming responses -->
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-openai-usage" />
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-cache-store" />
    </outbound>

    <on-error>
        <base />
        <!-- Capture 429s as warnings before optionally short-circuiting on 5xx -->
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-throttling-events" />
        <include-fragment fragment-id="ai-gateway-mock-fallback" />
    </on-error>
</policies>

metadata.description: Existing APIM service name.
Names of the AOAI APIs to attach each product to (one or more).
Definition of the products / use-cases to onboard.

variables.productPolicyTemplate:
<!--
    Product-level policy template.

    Each APIM Product (one per business app / use-case in this demo) sets two
    variables that the API-level policy then logs and emits as custom metric
    dimensions. The Bicep `apim-products.bicep` module substitutes the
    `__APP_ID__`, `__USE_CASE__`, `__TPM_LIMIT__` placeholders before applying.

    The product policy ALSO sets a tighter per-product token-per-minute limit
    so the demo can show different SKUs / tiers throttling independently.
-->
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />
        <set-variable name="appId" value="__APP_ID__" />
        <set-variable name="useCase" value="__USE_CASE__" />

        <!-- Per-product TPM ceiling. Counter is keyed by subscription so each
             onboarded subscription gets its own bucket within the product. -->
        <azure-openai-token-limit
            counter-key="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")"
            tokens-per-minute="__TPM_LIMIT__"
            estimate-prompt-tokens="false" />
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <base />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
        <base />
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
        <base />
    </on-error>
</policies>
